s1_reasoning: The computational kernel is memory-bound due to: 1) Irregular access pattern in the inner loop (A[j][k] causes poor spatial locality), 2) The j <= i condition prevents full vectorization and efficient cache utilization, 3) The triple nested loop has O(N²M) operations but accesses O(N² + NM) data, leading to low arithmetic intensity.

hotspots.reason: Triply nested loops performing O(N²M) operations with irregular memory access patterns (j <= i condition). The inner loops access A[i][k] and A[j][k] with strided memory access for A[j][k].
Double nested loops initializing arrays with O(N² + NM) operations, but only executed once.

bottleneck_type.reasoning: The computational kernel is memory-bound due to: 1) Irregular access pattern in the inner loop (A[j][k] causes poor spatial locality), 2) The j <= i condition prevents full vectorization and efficient cache utilization, 3) The triple nested loop has O(N²M) operations but accesses O(N² + NM) data, leading to low arithmetic intensity.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The SYRK operation is a level-3 BLAS operation with substantial parallelism (O(N²M) operations). GPUs excel at such regular matrix operations, though the triangular nature requires some attention.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use cuBLAS dsyrk for NVIDIA GPUs or rocBLAS for AMD GPUs. For custom implementation: 1) Map i-loop to thread blocks, 2) Use shared memory for A tiles, 3) Handle triangular condition with thread masking, 4) Use optimized warp-level primitives for reduction.
